summary.scholar: On November 19, 1863, Lincoln delivered a brief address at the dedication of the Soldiers National Cemetery at Gettysburg, redefining the purpose of the war in roughly 270 words.
summary.explorer: On November 19, 1863, Lincoln gave a short but powerful speech at Gettysburg to honor the soldiers who died there.

significance.scholar: The Gettysburg Address became one of the most quoted speeches in American history, reframing the war as a struggle to fulfill the promise of equality.
significance.explorer: Even though it was very short, the Gettysburg Address became one of the most famous speeches ever given.
